CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2.brief: An instance of data type Nef_polyhedron_S2 <Traits> is a subset of the sphere $ S_2$ that is the result of forming complements and intersections starting from a finite set H of halfspaces bounded by a plane containing the origin.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2.detailed: Halfspaces correspond to hemispheres of $ S_2$ and are therefore modeled by oriented great circles of type Sphere_circle . Nef_polyhedron_S2 is closed under all binary set operations intersection , union , difference , complement and under the topological operations boundary , closure , and interior . Parameters template < class NefPolyhedronTraits_S2, class NefPolyhedronItems_S2 = CGAL::SM_items, class Nef_polyhedronMarks = bool > class Nef_polyhedron_S2 ; The first parameter requires one of the following exact kernels: Homogeneous , Simple_homogeneous parametrized with Gmpz , leda_integer or any other number type modeling $\mathbb{Z}$ , or Cartesian , Simple_cartesian parametrized with Gmpq , leda_rational , Quotient<Gmpz> or any other number type modeling $\mathbb{Q}$ . The second parameter and the third parameter are for future considerations. Neither NefPolyhedronItems_S2 nor Nef_polyhedronMarks is specified, yet. Do not use other than the default types for these two template parameters. Exploration - Point location - Ray shooting As Nef polyhedra are the result of forming complements and intersections starting from a set H of halfspaces that are defined by oriented lines in the plane, they can be represented by an attributed plane map $ M = (V,E,F)$ . For topological queries within M the following types and operations allow exploration access to this structure. Input and Output A Nef polyhedron N can be visualized in an OpenGL window. The output operator is defined in the file CGAL/IO/Nef_polyhedron_2_Window-stream.h . Implementation Nef polyhedra are implemented on top of a halfedge data structure and use linear space in the number of vertices, edges and facets. Operations like empty take constant time. The operations clear() , complement() , interior() , closure() , boundary() , regularization() , input and output take linear time. All binary set operations and comparison operations take time $O(n \log n)$ where $ n$ is the size of the output plus the size of the input. The point location and ray shooting operations are implemented in the naive way. The operations run in linear query time without any preprocessing.

members.name: SHalfedge_around_svertex_const_circulator
members.brief: circulating the adjacency list of an svertex v .

members.name: SHalfedge_around_sface_const_circulator
members.brief: circulating the sface cycle of an sface f .

members.name: SFace_cycle_const_iterator
members.brief: iterating all sface cycles of an sface f .
members.detailed: The iterator has method bool is_svertex() , bool is_shalfedge() , bool is_shalfloop() , and can be converted to the corresponding handles SVertex_const_handle , SHalfedge_const_handle , or SHalfloop_const_handle .

members.name: Object_handle
members.brief: a generic handle to an object of the underlying plane map.
members.detailed: The kind of object (vertex, halfedge, face) can be determined and the object can be assigned to a corresponding handle by the three functions: bool assign(Vertex_const_handle& h, Object_handle ) bool assign(Halfedge_const_handle& h, Object_handle ) bool assign(Face_const_handle& h, Object_handle ) where each function returns true iff the assignment to h was done.

members.name: Nef_polyhedron_S2
members.brief: creates an instance N of type Nef_polyhedron_S2 <K> and initializes it to the empty set if sphere == EMPTY and to the whole sphere if sphere == COMPLETE .

members.name: Nef_polyhedron_S2
members.brief: creates a Nef polyhedron N containing the half-sphere left of c including c if circle== INCLUDED , excluding c if circle== EXCLUDED .

members.name: Nef_polyhedron_S2
members.brief: creates a Nef polyhedron N from the set of sphere segments in the iterator range [first,beyond) .
members.detailed: If the set of sphere segments is a simple polygon that separates the sphere surface into two regions, then the polygonal region that is left of the segment *first is selected. The polygonal region includes its boundary if b = INCLUDED and excludes the boundary otherwise. Forward_iterator has to be an iterator with value type Sphere_segment .

members.name: clear
members.brief: makes N the empty set if plane == EMPTY and the full plane if plane == COMPLETE .

members.name: is_empty
members.brief: returns true if N is empty, false otherwise.

members.name: is_sphere
members.brief: returns true if N is the whole sphere, false otherwise.

members.name: contains
members.brief: returns true iff the object h is contained in the set represented by N .

members.name: contained_in_boundary
members.brief: returns true iff the object h is contained in the $ 1$ -skeleton of N .

members.name: locate
members.brief: returns a generic handle h to an object (face, halfedge, vertex) of the underlying plane map that contains the point p in its relative interior.
members.detailed: The point p is contained in the set represented by N if N.contains(h) is true. The location mode flag m allows one to choose between different point location strategies.

members.name: ray_shoot
members.brief: returns a handle h with N.contains(h) that can be converted to a Vertex_/Halfedge_/Face_const_handle as described above.
members.detailed: The object returned is intersected by the ray starting in p with direction d and has minimal distance to p . The operation returns an empty Object_handle if the ray shoot along d does not hit any object h of N with N.contains(h) .

members.name: ray_shoot_to_boundary
members.brief: returns a handle h that can be converted to a Vertex_/Halfedge_const_handle as described above.
members.detailed: The object returned is part of the $ 1$ -skeleton of N , intersected by the ray starting in p with direction d and has minimal distance to p . The operation returns an empty Object_handle if the ray shoot along d does not hit any $ 1$ -skeleton object h of N . The location mode flag m allows one to choose between different point location strategies.

members.name: complement
members.brief: returns the complement of N in the plane.

members.name: regularization
members.brief: returns the regularized polyhedron (closure of interior).

members.name: symmetric_difference
members.brief: returns the symmetric difference N - T $ \cup$ T - N .

members.name: number_of_sface_cycles
members.brief: returns the number of sface cycles.

members.name: number_of_connected_components
members.brief: calculates the number of connected components of P .

members.name: print_statistics
members.brief: print the statistics of P : the number of vertices, edges, and faces.

members.name: check_integrity_and_topological_planarity
members.brief: checks the link structure and the genus of P .

members.name: has_shalfloop
members.brief: The list of all objects can be accessed via iterator ranges.
members.detailed: For comfortable iteration we also provide iterations macros. The iterator range access operations are of the following kind: SVertex_iterator svertices_begin()/svertices_end() SHalfedge_iterator shalfedges_begin()/shalfedges_end() SHalfloop_iterator shalfloops_begin()/shalfloops_end() SFace_iterator sfaces_begin()/sfaces_end() The macros are then CGAL_forall_svertices(v,M) , CGAL_forall_shalfedges(e,M) , CGAL_forall_sfaces(f,M) , CGAL_forall_sface_cycles_of(fc,F) where M is a sphere map and F is a sface. returns true iff there is a shalfloop.

members.name: equal_as_sets
members.brief: returns true iff c1 and c2 are equal as unoriented circles.
members.argsstring: (const Nef_polyhedron_S2< Traits >::Sphere_circle c1, const Nef_polyhedron_S2< Traits >::Sphere_circle c2)

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace.brief: Figures figureNefS2SVertexIncidences and figureNefS2SHalfloopIncidences illustrate the incidences of an sface.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace.detailed: An sface is described by its boundaries. An entry item to each boundary cycle can be accessed using the iterator range ( sface_cycles_begin() / sface_cycles_end() ). Additionally, Nef_polyhedron_S2 provides the macro CGAL_forall_sface_cycles_of . The iterators are of type SFace_cycle_const_iterator and represent either a shalfedge, a shalfloop, or a svertex. Creation There is no need for a user to create a SFace explicitly. The class Nef_polyhedron_S2 <Traits> manages the needed sfaces internally. CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2 CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SVertex

members.name: SFace_cycle_const_iterator
members.brief: const iterator over the entries to all sface cycles of a sface .

members.name: sface_cycle_begin
members.brief: iterator over the entries to all sface cycles of the sface .

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace_cycle_ite.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace_cycle_iterator
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace_cycle_ite.brief: The type SFace_cycle_iterator iterates over a list of Object_handles .
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace_cycle_ite.detailed: Each item of that list can either be assigned to SVertex_handle , SHalfedge_handle or SHalfloop_handle . To find out which of these assignment works out, the member functions is_svertex() , is_shalfedge() and is_shalfloop() are provided. CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SVertex CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfedge CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfloop

members.name: is_svertex
members.brief: returns true if the iterator represents a SVertex_handle .

members.name: is_shalfedge
members.brief: returns true if the iterator represents a SHalfedge_handle .

members.name: is_shalfloop
members.brief: returns true if the iterator represents a SHalfloop_handle .

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfedge.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfedge
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfedge.brief: A shalfedge is a great arc on a sphere map.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfedge.detailed: The figure below depicts the relationship between a shalfedge and its incident shalfedges, svertices, and sfaces on a sphere map. A shalfedge is an oriented sedge between two svertices. It is always paired with a shalfedge pointing in the opposite direction. The twin() member function returns this shalfedge of opposite orientation. Incidences of an SHalfedge Incidences of an SHalfedge The snext() member function points to the successor shalfedge around this sface while the sprev() member function points to the preceding shalfedge. An successive assignments of the form se = se-> snext() cycles counterclockwise around the sface (or hole). Similarly, the successive assignments of the form se = se-> snext() -> twin() cycle clockwise around the svertex and traverse all halfedges incident to this svertex. The assignment se = se-> cyclic_adj_succ() can be used as a shortcut. A const circulator is provided for each of the two circular orders. The circulators are bidirectional and assignable to SHalfedge_const_handle . Creation There is no need for a user to create a SHalfedge explicitly. The class Nef_polyhedron_S2 <Traits> manages the needed shalfedges internally. CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SVertex CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: sphere circle type stored in SHalfedge .

members.name: circle
members.brief: the sphere circle of the sedge .

members.name: source
members.brief: the source svertex of the sedge .

members.name: sprev
members.brief: the SHalfedge previous to the sedge in a sface cycle.

members.name: snext
members.brief: the next SHalfedge of the sedge in a sface cycle.

members.brief: the edge before the sedge in the cyclic ordered adjacency list of source() .

members.name: cyclic_adj_succ
members.brief: the edge after the sedge in the cyclic ordered adjacency list of source() .

members.name: incident_sface
members.brief: the incident sface of the sedge .

members.brief: determines whether the sedge is in an outer sface cycle.

members.name: in_inner_sface_cycle
members.brief: determines whether the sedge is in an inner sface cycle.

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfloop.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfloop
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfloop.brief: A sloop is a great circle on a sphere.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SHalfloop.detailed: A shalfloop is an oriented sloop. It is always paired with a shalfloop whose supporting Sphere_circle is pointing in the opposite direction. The twin() member function returns this shalfloop of opposite orientation. Each Nef_polyhedron_S2 can only have one sloop (resp. two shalfloops). The figure below depicts the relationship between a shalfloop and sfaces on a sphere map. Incidences of an SHalfloop Incidences of an SHalfloop Creation There is no need for a user to create a SHalfloop explicitly. The class Nef_polyhedron_S2 <Traits> manages the needed shalfloops internally. CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SFace CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: sphere circle type stored in SHalfloop .

members.name: circle
members.brief: the sphere circle of the halfloop.

members.name: incident_sface
members.brief: the incident sface of the halfloop.

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle.brief: An object c of type Sphere_circle is an oriented great circle on the surface of a unit sphere.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_circle.detailed: Such circles correspond to the intersection of an oriented plane (that contains the origin) and the surface of $ S_2$ . The orientation of the great circle is that of a counterclockwise walk along the circle as seen from the positive halfspace of the oriented plane.

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: If $ p$ and $ q$ are opposite of each other, then we create the unique great circle on $ S_2$ which contains p and q.
members.detailed: This circle is oriented such that a walk along c meets $ p$ just before the shorter segment between $ p$ and $ q$ . If $ p$ and $ q$ are opposite of each other then we create any great circle that contains $ p$ and $ q$ .

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: creates the circle corresponding to the plane h .

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: creates the circle orthogonal to the vector $ (x,y,z)$ .

members.name: Sphere_circle
members.brief: creates a great circle orthogonal to $ c$ that contains $ p$ .
members.detailed: $ p$ is not part of $ c$ .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Returns a sphere circle in the opposite direction of c .

members.name: has_on
members.brief: returns true iff c contains p .

members.name: orthogonal_pole
members.brief: returns the point that is the pole of the hemisphere left of c .

members.name: equal_as_sets
members.brief: returns true iff c1 and c2 are equal as unoriented circles.
members.argsstring: (const Nef_polyhedron_S2< Traits >::Sphere_circle c1, const Nef_polyhedron_S2< Traits >::Sphere_circle c2)

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_point.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_point
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_point.brief: An object p of type Sphere_point <R> is a point on the surface of a unit sphere.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_point.detailed: Such points correspond to the nontrivial directions in space and similarly to the equivalence classes of all nontrivial vectors under normalization. Operations Access to the coordinates is provided by the following operations. Note that the vector $ (x,y,z)$ is not normalized.

members.name: Sphere_point
members.brief: creates a sphere point corresponding to the point of intersection of the ray starting at the origin in direction $ (x,y,z)$ and the surface of $ S_2$ .

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_segment.name: CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_segment
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_segment.brief: An object s of type Sphere_segment is a segment in the surface of a unit sphere that is part of a great circle through the origin.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::Sphere_segment.detailed: Sphere segments are represented by two sphere points $ p$ and $ q$ plus an oriented plane $ h$ that contains $ p$ and $ q$ . The plane determines the sphere segment as follows. Let $ c$ be the circle in the intersection of $ h$ and $ S_2$ . Then $ s$ is that part of $ c$ that is swept, when we rotate $ p$ into $ q$ in counterclockwise rotation around the normal vector of $ h$ as seen from the positive halfspace.

members.name: Sphere_segment
members.brief: creates a spherical segment spanning the shorter arc from p1 to p2 if shorter_arc == true .
members.detailed: Otherwise the longer arc is created. p1 != p2 and p1 != p2.opposite() .

members.name: Sphere_segment
members.brief: creates a spherical segment spanning the arc from p1 to p2 as part of the oriented circle c ( p1 == p2 or p1 == p2.opposite() are possible.)
members.detailed: p1 and p2 are contained in c .

members.name: Sphere_segment
members.brief: creates the spherical segment as part of c1 that is part of the halfsphere left of the oriented circle c2 .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: returns the spherical segment oriented from target() to source() with the same point set as s .

members.name: complement
members.brief: returns the spherical segment oriented from target() to source() with the point set completing s to a full circle.

members.name: is_short
members.brief: a segment is short iff it is shorter than a half-circle.

members.name: is_long
members.brief: a segment is long iff it is longer than a half-circle.

members.name: is_degenerate
members.brief: return true iff s is degenerate, i.e. source and target are the same.

members.name: is_halfcircle
members.brief: return true iff s is a perfect half-circle, i.e. source() .antipode == target() .

members.name: has_on
members.brief: return true iff s contains p .

members.name: has_in_relative_interior
members.brief: return true iff s contains p in its relative interior.

CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SVertex.brief: Figure figureNefS2SVertexIncidences illustrates the incidence of a svertex on a sphere map.
CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_S2::SVertex.detailed: The member function out_sedge() returns the first outgoing shalfedge, and incident_sface() returns the incident sface. Creation There is no need for a user to create a SVertex explicitly. The class Nef_polyhedron_S2 <Traits> manages the needed svertices internally. PkgNefS2Ref.name: PkgNefS2Ref
PkgNefS2Ref.detailed: Peter Hachenberger and Lutz Kettner This package offers the equivalent to 2D Nef Polygons in the plane. Here halfplanes correspond to half spheres delimited by great circles. Introduced in: CGAL 3.1 Depends on: PkgNef2 BibTeX: cgal:hk-bonpes2-26a License: GPL Nef polyhedra are defined as a subset of the d-dimensional space obtained by a finite number of set complement and set intersection operations on halfspaces. Due to the fact that all other binary set operations like union, difference and symmetric difference can be reduced to intersection and complement calculations, Nef polyhedra are also closed under those operations. Also, Nef polyhedra are closed under topological unary set operations. Given a Nef polyhedron one can determine its interior, its boundary, and its closure. Additionally, a d-dimensional Nef polyhedron has the property, that its boundary is a (d-1)-dimensional Nef polyhedron. This property can be used as a way to represent 3-dimensional Nef polyhedra by means of planar Nef polyhedra. This is done by intersecting the neighborhood of a vertex in a 3D Nef polyhedron with an $ \epsilon$ -sphere. The result is a planar Nef polyhedron embedded on the sphere. The intersection of a halfspace going through the center of the $ \epsilon$ -sphere, with the $ \epsilon$ -sphere, results in a halfsphere which is bounded by a great circle. A binary operation of two halfspheres cuts the great circles into great arcs. The incidence structure of planar Nef polyhedra can be reused. The items are denoted as svertex , shalfedge and sface , analogous to their counterparts in CGAL::Nef_polyhedron_2 . Additionally, there is the shalfloop representing the great circles. Classified Reference Pages
